package service

import (
	"fmt"
	"gorm.io/gorm"
)

// Positive scenario for service_method_returns_map_string_any_output: risky concrete example for this rule.
// Rule intent: Services that return dynamic maps instead of typed result contracts.
type CaseServiceMethodReturnsMapStringAnyOutputModel struct {
	ID       string `gorm:"column:id" json:"id"`
	TenantID string `gorm:"column:tenant_id" json:"tenant_id"`
	Status   string `gorm:"column:status" json:"status"`
	Amount   int    `gorm:"column:amount" json:"amount"`
}

type CaseServiceMethodReturnsMapStringAnyOutputRepository struct {
	db *gorm.DB
}

type CaseServiceMethodReturnsMapStringAnyOutputService struct {
	repo *CaseServiceMethodReturnsMapStringAnyOutputRepository
}

func (s *CaseServiceMethodReturnsMapStringAnyOutputService) GetSummary(id string) (map[string]any, error) {
	var model CaseServiceMethodReturnsMapStringAnyOutputModel
	if err := s.repo.db.Where("id = ?", id).First(&model).Error; err != nil {
		return nil, fmt.Errorf("record not found: %w", err)
	}

	result := map[string]any{
		"id":         model.ID,
		"tenant_id":  model.TenantID,
		"status":     model.Status,
		"amount":     model.Amount,
		"formatted":  fmt.Sprintf("%s - %s", model.ID, model.Status),
	}

	return result, nil
}
